theme: locator beacons for open plazas and narrow corridors
title: Locator Beacons: One Anchor Layout, Five Viewpoint Regimes
strategies: trivial: a fixed-radius ring of anchors evenly spaced by angle around the whole site's centroid -- angularly diverse from the centroid, but most of the site (plaza2, the corridor, the perimeter, the mouths) sees the ring nearly edge-on

greedy: iterated farthest-point sampling over all open cells (maximize pairwise Euclidean spacing) -- ignores line-of-sight and bearing entirely, so it wastes anchors on scenarios that are already easy and leaves the corridor's targets seeing only near-collinear anchors along the corridor's own axis

strong: seed with the same farthest-point spread, then run a worst-case-aware local EXCHANGE search that recomputes each target's true visibility-gated bearing-diversity score (the checker's own GDOP formula) and swaps anchors to reduce a LEXICOGRAPHIC (worst-scenario-mean, then sum-of-means) objective -- lets the search keep improving an under-served scenario even while a different scenario is still the reported bottleneck, which is exactly what is needed to place anchors where they actually break the corridor's collinearity (at bends/mouths) instead of spreading them by raw distance

other viable directions: an explicit two-phase design (solve each scenario's minimum-anchor-count cover separately, then merge/dedupe positions); simulated annealing directly on the worst-regime objective; treating each scenario's required bearing coverage as an angular set-cover and picking anchors that jointly cover the union
